_comment_target: Action-conditioned v3 (ActionRenderCrossAdapterV3, model_v3.py) on the full 802-clip droid_1k train split, FSDP. v3 = 3-layer render encoder + 2-layer action encoder, LayerNorm on both, 2 TRADITIONAL pre-norm transformer blocks (residual cross-attn + residual FFN, render Q / action KV), 2-layer projection ADDED to temb_base before time_proj. Standard PyTorch init throughout — NO zero-init, NO small-init; the residual blocks are the stability mechanism. This is the fix for the v2 collapse/divergence (see memory:project_v2_adapter_collapse). 17 frames @ 240x432, 30 epochs — direct A/B against the egowm 30-epoch baseline (run_20260507_052841, ~16.2 dB) at matched resolution + length. Validated by the 10-vid x 10-epoch smoke: val loss 1.52 -> 0.33 monotonic, grad_norm bounded.
_metadata_note: Full 802-row train split — matches the egowm baseline's metadata exactly.
_num_frames_note: 17 = matches egowm baseline + v2 runs. T_lat=5 post-patch frames x 30x54 spatial = 8100 tokens.
_cache_note: v3 17-frame cache. Cache key is data-only (data + num_frames + has_actions + action_field), NOT architecture-dependent — so this is data-identical to the v2 17-frame cache. Separate filename to avoid confusion; first launch MISSES, rebuilds ~30 min for 802 videos.
_save_note: save_every_n_epochs=10 (epochs 9,19,29 = 3 saves x ~13GB = ~39GB). Kept sparse because /work/nvme/becx is at ~886G/1000G soft with 3 other runs also saving.
_val_note: Held-out 10-scene set (scenes 10000-10009, never seen in training). Runs every 2 epochs at 4 fixed timesteps per sample, deterministic noise.
